title: CLAUDE.md
text: Collate order DESCRIPTION authoritative; load order status-constants.R → utils.R → domain.R → path-query.R → parse-phases.R → parse.R → accessors.R → canonical_join.R → zzz.R. R/parse.R / R/parse-phases.R - Main parsing logic: safe_parse_url(), scalar/vector wrappers option validation (parse.R) 13 decomposed per-phase helpers .prepare_url_for_curl() … .assemble_parse_result() (parse-phases.R) R/accessors.R - Public get_*() accessor functions, built shared .extract_from_urls() helper safe_parse_url() R/domain.R - Punycode helpers pslr query seam (.psl_registered_domain(), .psl_public_suffix(), .psl_suffix_extract(), .host_is_ace()) R/path-query.R - Low-level path normalization (._collapse_path_slashes(), ._remove_dot_segments(), ._strip_index_page(), ._encode_path_segments()) query-string parsing (._parse_query_string()) R/canonical_join.R - Dataset joining canonicalized URL keys (canonical_join()) R/status-constants.R - .STATUS_* parse-status constants .is_*_status() predicates (incl. .is_joinable_status() used canonical_join()) R/utils.R - %||% null-coalescing operator .spu_result_fields (single source truth safe_parse_urls() result columns) R/zzz.R - Package initialization (.onLoad), cache registry (.CACHE_REGISTRY), memoization caches, public cache API (rurl_clear_caches(), rurl_cache_info(), rurl_cache_config())

title: CLAUDE.md
text: .normalize_and_punycode() (R/domain.R) - IDNA/Punycode encoding NFC normalization, used host reconstruction .punycode_to_unicode() (R/domain.R) - per-label Punycode decoding Unicode (lenient puny_decode + iconv sanitization) .psl_registered_domain() / .psl_public_suffix() (R/domain.R) - thin wrappers pslr::registrable_domain() / pslr::public_suffix() .psl_suffix_extract() (R/domain.R) - full canonical decomposition (subdomain / domain / suffix / registrable_domain) via pslr::suffix_extract(), used make STRUCTURAL policy decisions (www-prefix subdomain-trim heuristics) one canonical spelling .host_is_ace() (R/domain.R) - TRUE host label xn-- -label; drives host_encoding = "keep" spelling choice .derive_domain_tld()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: rurl calls pslr fixed contract: source "" / "icann" / "private" maps 1:1 onto pslr section. output = "unicode" default (preserves rurl’s historical decoded-IDN output; pslr defaults ASCII -labels). Structural/decision callers keep default; emitted domain/TLD path (.derive_domain_tld()) instead selects spelling host_encoding (keep/idna/unicode), get_domain()/get_tld()/get_subdomain() mirror get_host(). unknown = "na" unknown TLD yields NA rather pslr’s implicit *. invalid = "na" malformed hosts yield NA instead erroring. Never use pslr session-global list switching (psl_use()) implement per-request behavior (per pslr PRD §12).

title: Getting Started with rurl
text: Use safe_parse_url() parse URLs robustly: protocol_handling argument controls schemes handled: "keep" (default; keeps current protocol prepends http:// missing) "none" (doesn’t add, remove, change protocols) "strip" (removes protocols) "http" (changes protocols http:// adds missing) "https" (changes protocols https:// adds missing)
code: safe_parse_url("https://sub.example.co.uk/path?q=1") #> $original_url #> [1] "https://sub.example.co.uk/path?q=1" #>  #> $scheme #> [1] "https" #>  #> $host #> [1] "sub.example.co.uk" #>  #> $port #> [1] NA #>  #> $path #> [1] "/path" #>  #> $query #> [1] "q=1" #>  #> $fragment #> [1] NA #>  #> $user #> [1] NA #>  #> $password #> [1] NA #>  #> $domain #> [1] "example.co.uk" #>  #> $tld #> [1] "co.uk" #>  #> $is_ip_host #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $clean_url #> [1] "https://sub.example.co.uk/path" #>  #> $parse_status #> [1] "ok"

title: Getting Started with rurl
text: Several functions, including safe_parse_url(), get_host(), get_clean_url(), support subdomain_levels_to_keep argument. allows fine-grained control many subdomain levels preserved host component URL, initial www_handling applied. NULL (Default): specific subdomain stripping performed beyond www_handling. 0: subdomains stripped. www_handling preserved added ‘www.’, remains (e.g., ‘www.sub.example.com’ becomes ‘www.example.com’; ‘sub.example.com’ becomes ‘example.com’). N > 0: Keeps N levels subdomains, counted right--left (closest registered domain), addition ‘www.’ prefix. examples demonstrating effect get_host(): effect get_clean_url(): Note get_domain() also accepts subdomain_levels_to_keep, change returned domain value. domain derived host specific host modification occurs. parameter influences host component might used parts safe_parse_url output, clean_url.
code: get_host(   "www.three.two.one.example.com",   subdomain_levels_to_keep = 0 ) # www_handling default is "none" #> [1] "www.example.com" # Expected: "www.example.com"  get_host(   "three.two.one.example.com",   www_handling = "strip",   subdomain_levels_to_keep = 0 ) #> [1] "example.com" # Expected: "example.com"  get_host("www.three.two.one.example.com", subdomain_levels_to_keep = 1) #> [1] "www.one.example.com" # Expected: "www.one.example.com"  get_host(   "three.two.one.example.com",   www_handling = "strip",   subdomain_levels_to_keep = 1 ) #> [1] "one.example.com" # Expected: "one.example.com"  get_host(   "www.three.two.one.example.com",   www_handling = "keep",   subdomain_levels_to_keep = 2 ) #> [1] "www.two.one.example.com" # Expected: "www.two.one.example.com" get_clean_url(   "http://www.deep.sub.example.com/some/path",   subdomain_levels_to_keep = 0,   www_handling = "keep" ) #> [1] "http://www.example.com/some/path" # yields http://www.example.com/some/path  get_clean_url(   "http://deep.sub.example.com/some/path",   subdomain_levels_to_keep = 1 ) #> [1] "http://sub.example.com/some/path" # yields http://sub.example.com/some/path

title: rurl — Parse, Clean, and Normalize URLs
text: safe_parse_url() core workhorse. returns parsed components normalized clean_url. clean_url normalized canonical key built scheme, host, path . Port, query, fragment, userinfo intentionally excluded — read dedicated components (get_port(), get_query(), get_fragment(), get_userinfo()) instead. path_encoding = "decode" path shown decoded, clean_url human-readable rather guaranteed URL-safe. Scheme-relative URL handling configurable: vectors, use safe_parse_urls():
code: library(rurl)  parsed <- safe_parse_url(   "HTTP://www.Example.com/a//b/../index.html?x=1#frag",   protocol_handling = "https",   www_handling = "strip",   case_handling = "lower_host",   trailing_slash_handling = "strip",   index_page_handling = "strip",   path_normalization = "both",   host_encoding = "idna",   path_encoding = "encode" )  parsed$clean_url #> [1] "https://example.com/a" parsed$parse_status #> [1] "ok" safe_parse_url("//example.com/path", scheme_relative_handling = "keep")$parse_status #> [1] "ok-scheme-relative" safe_parse_url("//example.com/path", scheme_relative_handling = "https")$clean_url #> [1] "https://example.com/path" safe_parse_urls(c("example.com", "https://www.example.com/path"))[, c("original_url", "clean_url", "parse_status")]

title: rurl — Parse, Clean, and Normalize URLs
text: safe_parse_url() get_clean_url() support controls: protocol_handling: keep, none, strip, http, https www_handling: none, strip, keep, if_no_subdomain case_handling: lower_host (default), keep, lower, upper trailing_slash_handling: none, keep, strip index_page_handling: keep, strip path_normalization: none, collapse_slashes, dot_segments, scheme_relative_handling: keep, http, https, error host_encoding: keep, idna, unicode path_encoding: keep, encode, decode subdomain_levels_to_keep: NULL, 0, N > 0 Subdomain retention applied www_handling: Host path encoding controls:
code: get_host("http://www.three.two.one.example.com", www_handling = "strip", subdomain_levels_to_keep = 1) #> [1] "one.example.com" get_clean_url("http://www.deep.sub.example.com/path", subdomain_levels_to_keep = 0) #> [1] "http://www.example.com/path" get_clean_url("http://münich.com/a%20b",               host_encoding = "idna",               path_encoding = "encode",               case_handling = "lower_host") #> [1] "http://xn--mnich-kva.com/a%20b"  get_clean_url("http://xn--mnich-kva.com/a%20b",               host_encoding = "unicode",               path_encoding = "decode",               case_handling = "keep") #> [1] "http://münich.com/a b"

title: rurl — Parse, Clean, and Normalize URLs
text: rurl memoizes URL parsing punycode round-trips speed repeated operations large URL vectors; PSL query caching lives pslr. Inspect, clear, configure caches: rurl_cache_config() covers three caches: full_parse, puny_encode, puny_decode. full_parse cache unbounded default (max_full_parse = Inf); set bound cap peak memory. puny_encode puny_decode caches unbounded design can disabled workloads many unique hosts.
code: rurl_cache_info()                          # entries / enabled / max per cache rurl_clear_caches()                        # free memory in a long-running session rurl_cache_config(max_full_parse = 1e5)    # bound the full-parse cache rurl_cache_config(puny_encode = FALSE)     # disable a cache entirely

title: Canonical Join of Two URL Sets (Base R Version) — canonical_join
code: canonical_join(   data_A,   data_B,   col_A = "URL",   col_B = "URL",   suffix_A = "_A",   suffix_B = "_B",   name_A = NULL,   name_B = NULL,   join = c("inner", "left", "right", "full"),   collision = c("first", "all", "error"),   on_parse_error = c("keep", "drop", "error"),   join_parse_status = c("ok", "ok_or_warning"),   ... )

title: Canonical Join of Two URL Sets (Base R Version) — canonical_join
text: data_A data frame containing URLs left side join. data_B data frame containing URLs right side join. col_A Character string, name column data_A contains URLs. Defaults "URL". col_B Character string, name column data_B contains URLs. Defaults "URL". suffix_A Character string, suffix append data_A columns (excluding URL column) output. Defaults "_A". suffix_B Character string, suffix append data_B columns (excluding URL column) output. Defaults "_B". name_A Character string, name output column holding original data_A URLs. Defaults NULL, case name derived data_A argument expression via deparse(substitute()). Supply explicit value stable output names piping passing anonymous inputs (e.g. canonical_join(df[df$x > 1, ], get_b())). name_B Character string, name output column holding original data_B URLs. Defaults NULL; behaves like name_A data_B. join Join type: "inner", "left", "right", "full". Defaults "inner". collision handle duplicate canonical keys within inputs. "first" keeps first row per key, "" keeps rows (many--many), "error" stops duplicates. Defaults "first". on_parse_error handle URLs fail canonicalization. "keep" retains unmatched rows (left/right/full joins), "drop" removes joining, "error" stops. Defaults "keep". join_parse_status parse statuses yield joinable canonical keys. "ok" (default) joins rows whose parse_status begins "ok" ("ok", "ok-ftp", "ok-scheme-relative"). "ok_or_warning" additionally treats parseable--suspicious warning-* statuses ("warning--tld", "warning-invalid-tld", "warning-public-suffix") joinable. Joining warning statuses can increase false-positive matches distinct hosts fail TLD derivation. ... Additional arguments forwarded safe_parse_urls, controlling canonicalization (e.g., protocol_handling, www_handling, trailing_slash_handling, index_page_handling, path_normalization, scheme_relative_handling, host_encoding, path_encoding).

title: Canonical Join of Two URL Sets (Base R Version) — canonical_join
text: data frame representing join. output includes: original URL columns (named via name_A / name_B, input expressions NULL). JoinKey: canonicalized URL used matching. columns data_A data_B suffixes applied. Returns empty data frame expected structure matches found inputs invalid.

title: Canonical Join of Two URL Sets (Base R Version) — canonical_join
code: A <- data.frame(   URL = c("http://Example.com/Page", "http://example.com/Other"),   ValA = 1:2, stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) B <- data.frame(   URL = c("https://www.example.com/Page/", "http://example.com/Miss"),   ValB = c("x", "y"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  canonical_join(   A, B,   protocol_handling = "strip",   www_handling = "strip",   case_handling = "lower_host",   trailing_slash_handling = "strip" ) #>                         A                             B          JoinKey ValA_A #> 1 http://Example.com/Page https://www.example.com/Page/ example.com/Page      1 #>   ValB_B #> 1      x

title: Get cleaned URLs — get_clean_url

title: Get cleaned URLs — get_clean_url
code: get_clean_url(   url,   protocol_handling = "keep",   www_handling = "none",   source = c("all", "private", "icann"),   case_handling = "lower_host",   trailing_slash_handling = "none",   index_page_handling = "keep",   path_normalization = "none",   scheme_relative_handling = "keep",   subdomain_levels_to_keep = NULL,   host_encoding = "keep",   path_encoding = "keep" )

title: Get cleaned URLs — get_clean_url
text: url character vector containing URLs parsed. protocol_handling character string specifying handle protocols. Defaults "keep". Regardless option, rurl processes authority-based URLs whose scheme one http, https, ftp, ftps; scheme-bearing input scheme (e.g. mailto:, tel:, ws:) yields parse_status = "error". Scheme inference () also requires input host-shaped: scheme-less string host (e.g. "asdfghjkl", "12345", "/path") non-canonical IP literal (integer/hex/octal/short forms, leading-zero octets like "192.168.010.1") rejected "error" rather scheme fabricated . "keep": supported scheme exists (http, https, ftp, ftps), used. scheme input host-shaped, "http://" added; otherwise input URL yields "error". "none": supported scheme exists, used. scheme, scheme used (scheme component NA). "strip": existing scheme removed (scheme component NA). "http": scheme forced "http". "https": scheme forced "https". www_handling character string specifying handle "www" www[number] prefixes host. Defaults "none". "none": (Default) Leaves host's www prefix (lack thereof) untouched. "strip": Removes "www." www[number]. prefix. "keep": Ensures host starts "www.". www[number]., normalized "www.". www prefix, "www." added. empty input host remains empty. "if_no_subdomain": host bare registered domain (e.g., "example.com"), "www." added. host already "www." www[number]. prefix, normalized "www." (e.g., "www1.example.com" becomes "www.example.com"; "www1.sub.example.com" becomes "www.sub.example.com"). non-www subdomain exists (e.g., "sub.example.com" normalized "www.sub.example.com"), host altered. empty input host remains empty. source PSL source use: "", "private", "icann". Subdomain trimming depends section consulted, pass source = "icann" exclude private suffixes (e.g. github.io). case_handling character string specifying handle case cleaned URL. Defaults "lower_host", RFC 3986 §6.2.2.1 normalization (scheme host case-insensitive folded lowercase; path case-sensitive preserved). "lower_host": (Default) Lowercases scheme host ; path keeps original casing. "keep": Preserves casing reconstructed URL. "lower": Converts cleaned URL lowercase. "upper": Converts cleaned URL uppercase. trailing_slash_handling character string specifying handle trailing slashes path component cleaned URL. Defaults "none". "none": (Default) specific handling applied. Path remains initial parsing. "keep": Ensures trailing slash. path exists end one, added. path just "/", kept. "strip": Removes trailing slash present, unless path solely "/". index_page_handling character string specifying handle index/default pages. Defaults "keep". "keep": (Default) Leave index/default page segments untouched. "strip": Remove trailing index.* default.* segment (case-insensitive). path_normalization normalize path structure. Defaults "none". rurl owns dot-segment resolution: path read input verbatim (libcurl's pre-normalized path), "none" preserves . / .. segments (//../b stays //../b) settings change . Resolution follows RFC 3986 section 5.2.4 acts literal ./.. segments — percent-encoded %2e normal path byte, never dot segment, never treated traversal. "none": (Default) normalization; dot slash structure preserved exactly written. "collapse_slashes": Collapse duplicate slashes path. "dot_segments": Resolve . .. segments per RFC 3986. "": Apply collapse_slashes dot_segments. scheme_relative_handling handle URLs starting "//". Defaults "keep". "keep": Parse using http return scheme NA set status "ok-scheme-relative". "http": Assume http parsing output. "https": Assume https parsing output. "error": Treat scheme-relative URLs invalid. subdomain_levels_to_keep integer NULL. Determines many levels subdomains kept, addition 'www.' prefix handled www_handling. NULL: (Default) specific subdomain stripping performed beyond www_handling. 0: subdomains stripped. www_handling preserved added 'www.', remains (e.g., 'www.sub.example.com' becomes 'www.example.com'; 'sub.example.com' becomes 'example.com'). N > 0: Keeps N levels subdomains, counted right--left (closest registered domain), addition 'www.' prefix. E.g., N=1, 'three.two.one.example.com' becomes 'one.example.com'; 'www.three.two.one.example.com' (post www_handling) becomes 'www.one.example.com'. host_encoding present host clean_url. Defaults "keep". "keep": Leave host parsed curl (may preserve original case). "idna": Convert Unicode host labels Punycode (IDNA) cleaned URL. "unicode": Decode Punycode labels Unicode cleaned URL. path_encoding handle percent-encoding path clean_url. Defaults "keep". "keep": Leave path percent-encoding untouched (path preserved written URL, %2F stays %2F rather decoding path-separating /). one normalization applied percent-hex case: two hex digits %XX triplet uppercased, %2f becomes %2F. RFC 3986 (section 6.2.2.1) canonicalization — two forms equivalent — makes paths compare equal canonical_join. Use "encode" additionally normalize bytes encoded. "encode": Normalize decoding first, percent-encoding segment (slashes preserved). "decode": Percent-decode UTF-8 sequences path.

title: Get cleaned URLs — get_clean_url
text: character vector cleaned URLs.

title: Get cleaned URLs — get_clean_url
code: get_clean_url("Example.COM/Path") # Default lower_host: host folds, path kept #> [1] "http://example.com/Path" get_clean_url(   "Example.COM/Path",   case_handling = "keep",   trailing_slash_handling = "keep" ) #> [1] "http://Example.COM/Path/" get_clean_url(   "Example.COM/Path/",   case_handling = "upper",   trailing_slash_handling = "strip" ) #> [1] "HTTP://EXAMPLE.COM/PATH" get_clean_url("http://example.com", www_handling = "strip") #> [1] "http://example.com/" get_clean_url(   "http://deep.sub.domain.example.com/path",   subdomain_levels_to_keep = 0 ) #> [1] "http://example.com/path" # -> "http://example.com/path" get_clean_url(   "http://www.deep.sub.domain.example.com/path",   subdomain_levels_to_keep = 1,   www_handling = "strip" ) #> [1] "http://domain.example.com/path" # -> "http://domain.example.com/path" get_clean_url(   "http://www.deep.sub.domain.example.com/path",   subdomain_levels_to_keep = 1,   www_handling = "keep" ) #> [1] "http://www.domain.example.com/path" # -> "http://www.domain.example.com/path"

title: Configure the rurl memoization caches — rurl_cache_config

title: Parse a URL comprehensively, extracting and deriving all relevant components. — safe_parse_url
text: url single URL string parsed. vectors, use safe_parse_urls. protocol_handling character string specifying handle protocols. Defaults "keep". Regardless option, rurl processes authority-based URLs whose scheme one http, https, ftp, ftps; scheme-bearing input scheme (e.g. mailto:, tel:, ws:) yields parse_status = "error". Scheme inference () also requires input host-shaped: scheme-less string host (e.g. "asdfghjkl", "12345", "/path") non-canonical IP literal (integer/hex/octal/short forms, leading-zero octets like "192.168.010.1") rejected "error" rather scheme fabricated . "keep": supported scheme exists (http, https, ftp, ftps), used. scheme input host-shaped, "http://" added; otherwise input URL yields "error". "none": supported scheme exists, used. scheme, scheme used (scheme component NA). "strip": existing scheme removed (scheme component NA). "http": scheme forced "http". "https": scheme forced "https". www_handling character string specifying handle "www" www[number] prefixes host. Defaults "none". "none": (Default) Leaves host's www prefix (lack thereof) untouched. "strip": Removes "www." www[number]. prefix. "keep": Ensures host starts "www.". www[number]., normalized "www.". www prefix, "www." added. empty input host remains empty. "if_no_subdomain": host bare registered domain (e.g., "example.com"), "www." added. host already "www." www[number]. prefix, normalized "www." (e.g., "www1.example.com" becomes "www.example.com"; "www1.sub.example.com" becomes "www.sub.example.com"). non-www subdomain exists (e.g., "sub.example.com" normalized "www.sub.example.com"), host altered. empty input host remains empty. tld_source TLD source use TLD extraction: "", "icann", "private". Defaults "". case_handling character string specifying handle case cleaned URL. Defaults "lower_host", RFC 3986 §6.2.2.1 normalization (scheme host case-insensitive folded lowercase; path case-sensitive preserved). "lower_host": (Default) Lowercases scheme host ; path keeps original casing. "keep": Preserves casing reconstructed URL. "lower": Converts cleaned URL lowercase. "upper": Converts cleaned URL uppercase. trailing_slash_handling character string specifying handle trailing slashes path component cleaned URL. Defaults "none". "none": (Default) specific handling applied. Path remains initial parsing. "keep": Ensures trailing slash. path exists end one, added. path just "/", kept. "strip": Removes trailing slash present, unless path solely "/". index_page_handling character string specifying handle index/default pages. Defaults "keep". "keep": (Default) Leave index/default page segments untouched. "strip": Remove trailing index.* default.* segment (case-insensitive). path_normalization normalize path structure. Defaults "none". rurl owns dot-segment resolution: path read input verbatim (libcurl's pre-normalized path), "none" preserves . / .. segments (//../b stays //../b) settings change . Resolution follows RFC 3986 section 5.2.4 acts literal ./.. segments — percent-encoded %2e normal path byte, never dot segment, never treated traversal. "none": (Default) normalization; dot slash structure preserved exactly written. "collapse_slashes": Collapse duplicate slashes path. "dot_segments": Resolve . .. segments per RFC 3986. "": Apply collapse_slashes dot_segments. scheme_relative_handling handle URLs starting "//". Defaults "keep". "keep": Parse using http return scheme NA set status "ok-scheme-relative". "http": Assume http parsing output. "https": Assume https parsing output. "error": Treat scheme-relative URLs invalid. subdomain_levels_to_keep integer NULL. Determines many levels subdomains kept, addition 'www.' prefix handled www_handling. NULL: (Default) specific subdomain stripping performed beyond www_handling. 0: subdomains stripped. www_handling preserved added 'www.', remains (e.g., 'www.sub.example.com' becomes 'www.example.com'; 'sub.example.com' becomes 'example.com'). N > 0: Keeps N levels subdomains, counted right--left (closest registered domain), addition 'www.' prefix. E.g., N=1, 'three.two.one.example.com' becomes 'one.example.com'; 'www.three.two.one.example.com' (post www_handling) becomes 'www.one.example.com'. host_encoding present host clean_url. Defaults "keep". "keep": Leave host parsed curl (may preserve original case). "idna": Convert Unicode host labels Punycode (IDNA) cleaned URL. "unicode": Decode Punycode labels Unicode cleaned URL. path_encoding handle percent-encoding path clean_url. Defaults "keep". "keep": Leave path percent-encoding untouched (path preserved written URL, %2F stays %2F rather decoding path-separating /). one normalization applied percent-hex case: two hex digits %XX triplet uppercased, %2f becomes %2F. RFC 3986 (section 6.2.2.1) canonicalization — two forms equivalent — makes paths compare equal canonical_join. Use "encode" additionally normalize bytes encoded. "encode": Normalize decoding first, percent-encoding segment (slashes preserved). "decode": Percent-decode UTF-8 sequences path. query_handling character string controlling whether () query string included clean_url. Defaults "drop", preserves historical query-free clean_url. raw query result field never affected option — always reports faithful original query. "drop": (Default) clean_url carries query, exactly . "filter": Keep contentful params, dropping known trackers via built-denylist (e.g. utm_*, fbclid, gclid). params_drop extends denylist; params_keep rescues names (winning denylist empty-dropping). "allow": Keep params whose names match params_keep; others dropped. params_keep inclusion criterion , empty-rescue. "keep": Keep every param, re-encoded canonical form (verbatim original — stays query field). every non-"drop" mode surviving query re-encoded canonically (uppercase percent-hex, spaces %20) appended path. query intentionally EXEMPT case_handling (query values case-sensitive — tokens, IDs, signatures), case_handling = "lower" "upper" clean_url longer uniformly cased: scheme/host/path fold query keeps original case. clean_url canonical_join key, non-"drop" mode also brings query join key (?id=1 ?id=2 stop collapsing, utm-differences still collapse "filter"). params_keep Character vector parameter-name globs (* special), NULL (default). "filter" mode rescue list; "allow" mode allowlist. Ignored "drop"/"keep". params_drop Character vector parameter-name globs add built-denylist "filter" mode, NULL (default). Ignored "drop"/"allow"/"keep". sort_params Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, surviving params stably sorted decoded key. Active "filter"/"allow"/"keep". empty_param_handling One "keep" (default) "drop". "drop" removes empty-valued params (e.g. ?ref=), except rescued params_keep "filter" mode. params_case_sensitive Logical (default FALSE). Controls whether denylist params_keep/params_drop matching case-sensitive. decode_plus Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, + query values treated space (HTML-form decoding) percent-decoding. FALSE keeps + literal (RFC 3986 generic behavior).

title: Parse a URL comprehensively, extracting and deriving all relevant components. — safe_parse_url
text: named list following components: original_url: original URL string provided. scheme: scheme (e.g., "http", "https"). host: host (e.g., "www.example.com"). NA host becomes empty processing. port: port number. path: path component (e.g., "/path//resource"). query: raw query string written URL, preserved byte--byte (e.g., "name=value"); percent-decoded. present-- empty query (e.g. trailing "?") reported NA. fragment: fragment identifier written URL (e.g., "section"); percent-decoded. Empty reported NA. user: user name authentication, written URL; percent-decoded. Empty reported NA. password: password authentication, written URL; percent-decoded. Empty reported NA. domain: registered domain name (e.g., "example.com"). NA host IP, empty, derivation fails. tld: top-level domain (e.g., "com"). NA host IP, empty, derivation fails. is_ip_host: Logical, TRUE host IP address. clean_url: normalized canonical key reconstructed scheme, host, path, processing case handling applied. query included query_handling != "drop" (default "drop", default query excluded); included filtered/canonicalized per query options appended case-unfolded. Port, fragment, userinfo always excluded (use dedicated components retrieve ). path_encoding = "decode" path shown decoded, clean_url human-readable rather guaranteed URL-safe. NA host empty/NA. parse_status: Character string indicating parsing outcome ("ok", "ok-ftp", "ok-scheme-relative", "error", "warning--tld", "warning-invalid-tld", "warning-public-suffix", "warning-userinfo"). "warning-userinfo" marks scheme-less input carrying userinfo (e.g. "user@example.com"): host/domain/tld/user still resolve, clean_url NA (rurl fabricate canonical URL ambiguous, email-shaped, scheme-less string). Returns NULL URL fundamentally unparseable (e.g., NA, empty) uses disallowed scheme.

title: Parse multiple URLs and return a data.frame of components — safe_parse_urls

code: safe_parse_urls(   url,   protocol_handling = c("keep", "none", "strip", "http", "https"),   www_handling = c("none", "strip", "keep", "if_no_subdomain"),   tld_source = c("all", "private", "icann"),   case_handling = c("lower_host", "keep", "lower", "upper"),   trailing_slash_handling = c("none", "keep", "strip"),   index_page_handling = c("keep", "strip"),   path_normalization = c("none", "collapse_slashes", "dot_segments", "both"),   scheme_relative_handling = c("keep", "http", "https", "error"),   subdomain_levels_to_keep = NULL,   host_encoding = c("keep", "idna", "unicode"),   path_encoding = c("keep", "encode", "decode"),   query_handling = c("drop", "filter", "allow", "keep"),   params_keep = NULL,   params_drop = NULL,   sort_params = FALSE,   empty_param_handling = c("keep", "drop"),   params_case_sensitive = FALSE,   decode_plus = FALSE )

title: Parse multiple URLs and return a data.frame of components — safe_parse_urls
text: url character vector URLs parsed. protocol_handling character string specifying handle protocols. Defaults "keep". Regardless option, rurl processes authority-based URLs whose scheme one http, https, ftp, ftps; scheme-bearing input scheme (e.g. mailto:, tel:, ws:) yields parse_status = "error". Scheme inference () also requires input host-shaped: scheme-less string host (e.g. "asdfghjkl", "12345", "/path") non-canonical IP literal (integer/hex/octal/short forms, leading-zero octets like "192.168.010.1") rejected "error" rather scheme fabricated . "keep": supported scheme exists (http, https, ftp, ftps), used. scheme input host-shaped, "http://" added; otherwise input URL yields "error". "none": supported scheme exists, used. scheme, scheme used (scheme component NA). "strip": existing scheme removed (scheme component NA). "http": scheme forced "http". "https": scheme forced "https". www_handling character string specifying handle "www" www[number] prefixes host. Defaults "none". "none": (Default) Leaves host's www prefix (lack thereof) untouched. "strip": Removes "www." www[number]. prefix. "keep": Ensures host starts "www.". www[number]., normalized "www.". www prefix, "www." added. empty input host remains empty. "if_no_subdomain": host bare registered domain (e.g., "example.com"), "www." added. host already "www." www[number]. prefix, normalized "www." (e.g., "www1.example.com" becomes "www.example.com"; "www1.sub.example.com" becomes "www.sub.example.com"). non-www subdomain exists (e.g., "sub.example.com" normalized "www.sub.example.com"), host altered. empty input host remains empty. tld_source TLD source use TLD extraction: "", "icann", "private". Defaults "". case_handling character string specifying handle case cleaned URL. Defaults "lower_host", RFC 3986 §6.2.2.1 normalization (scheme host case-insensitive folded lowercase; path case-sensitive preserved). "lower_host": (Default) Lowercases scheme host ; path keeps original casing. "keep": Preserves casing reconstructed URL. "lower": Converts cleaned URL lowercase. "upper": Converts cleaned URL uppercase. trailing_slash_handling character string specifying handle trailing slashes path component cleaned URL. Defaults "none". "none": (Default) specific handling applied. Path remains initial parsing. "keep": Ensures trailing slash. path exists end one, added. path just "/", kept. "strip": Removes trailing slash present, unless path solely "/". index_page_handling character string specifying handle index/default pages. Defaults "keep". "keep": (Default) Leave index/default page segments untouched. "strip": Remove trailing index.* default.* segment (case-insensitive). path_normalization normalize path structure. Defaults "none". rurl owns dot-segment resolution: path read input verbatim (libcurl's pre-normalized path), "none" preserves . / .. segments (//../b stays //../b) settings change . Resolution follows RFC 3986 section 5.2.4 acts literal ./.. segments — percent-encoded %2e normal path byte, never dot segment, never treated traversal. "none": (Default) normalization; dot slash structure preserved exactly written. "collapse_slashes": Collapse duplicate slashes path. "dot_segments": Resolve . .. segments per RFC 3986. "": Apply collapse_slashes dot_segments. scheme_relative_handling handle URLs starting "//". Defaults "keep". "keep": Parse using http return scheme NA set status "ok-scheme-relative". "http": Assume http parsing output. "https": Assume https parsing output. "error": Treat scheme-relative URLs invalid. subdomain_levels_to_keep integer NULL. Determines many levels subdomains kept, addition 'www.' prefix handled www_handling. NULL: (Default) specific subdomain stripping performed beyond www_handling. 0: subdomains stripped. www_handling preserved added 'www.', remains (e.g., 'www.sub.example.com' becomes 'www.example.com'; 'sub.example.com' becomes 'example.com'). N > 0: Keeps N levels subdomains, counted right--left (closest registered domain), addition 'www.' prefix. E.g., N=1, 'three.two.one.example.com' becomes 'one.example.com'; 'www.three.two.one.example.com' (post www_handling) becomes 'www.one.example.com'. host_encoding present host clean_url. Defaults "keep". "keep": Leave host parsed curl (may preserve original case). "idna": Convert Unicode host labels Punycode (IDNA) cleaned URL. "unicode": Decode Punycode labels Unicode cleaned URL. path_encoding handle percent-encoding path clean_url. Defaults "keep". "keep": Leave path percent-encoding untouched (path preserved written URL, %2F stays %2F rather decoding path-separating /). one normalization applied percent-hex case: two hex digits %XX triplet uppercased, %2f becomes %2F. RFC 3986 (section 6.2.2.1) canonicalization — two forms equivalent — makes paths compare equal canonical_join. Use "encode" additionally normalize bytes encoded. "encode": Normalize decoding first, percent-encoding segment (slashes preserved). "decode": Percent-decode UTF-8 sequences path. query_handling character string controlling whether () query string included clean_url. Defaults "drop", preserves historical query-free clean_url. raw query result field never affected option — always reports faithful original query. "drop": (Default) clean_url carries query, exactly . "filter": Keep contentful params, dropping known trackers via built-denylist (e.g. utm_*, fbclid, gclid). params_drop extends denylist; params_keep rescues names (winning denylist empty-dropping). "allow": Keep params whose names match params_keep; others dropped. params_keep inclusion criterion , empty-rescue. "keep": Keep every param, re-encoded canonical form (verbatim original — stays query field). every non-"drop" mode surviving query re-encoded canonically (uppercase percent-hex, spaces %20) appended path. query intentionally EXEMPT case_handling (query values case-sensitive — tokens, IDs, signatures), case_handling = "lower" "upper" clean_url longer uniformly cased: scheme/host/path fold query keeps original case. clean_url canonical_join key, non-"drop" mode also brings query join key (?id=1 ?id=2 stop collapsing, utm-differences still collapse "filter"). params_keep Character vector parameter-name globs (* special), NULL (default). "filter" mode rescue list; "allow" mode allowlist. Ignored "drop"/"keep". params_drop Character vector parameter-name globs add built-denylist "filter" mode, NULL (default). Ignored "drop"/"allow"/"keep". sort_params Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, surviving params stably sorted decoded key. Active "filter"/"allow"/"keep". empty_param_handling One "keep" (default) "drop". "drop" removes empty-valued params (e.g. ?ref=), except rescued params_keep "filter" mode. params_case_sensitive Logical (default FALSE). Controls whether denylist params_keep/params_drop matching case-sensitive. decode_plus Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, + query values treated space (HTML-form decoding) percent-decoding. FALSE keeps + literal (RFC 3986 generic behavior).

title: rurl (development version)
text: safe_parse_url() safe_parse_urls() gain opt-query-string handling clean_url (RURL-mzbnubrx). New query_handling option: "drop" (default — clean_url stays query-free, exactly ), "filter" (keep contentful params, drop known trackers utm_*, fbclid, gclid via built-denylist), "allow" (keep names params_keep), "keep" (keep every param, canonicalized). Supporting options: params_keep, params_drop (glob-aware, *-), sort_params, empty_param_handling, params_case_sensitive, decode_plus. raw query result field untouched — always reports faithful original. defaults preserve current output. canonical_join() forwards ... safe_parse_urls(), options also flow join key, non-"drop" mode makes ?id=1/?id=2 stop collapsing utm-differences still collapse "filter". clean_url query deliberately exempt case_handling (query values case-sensitive — tokens, IDs, signatures). case_handling = "lower" "upper" scheme/host/path fold appended query keeps original case, clean_url longer uniformly cased modes.

title: rurl (development version)
text: path_normalization = "none" (default) now genuinely lossless path structure (RURL-chdrlyci). request path read input verbatim rather libcurl’s pre-normalized path, ./.. segments preserved: "http://ex.com//../b" now yields clean_url "http://ex.com//../b" instead "http://ex.com/b". clean_url canonical_join key, dot-segment paths previously collided longer — pass path_normalization = "dot_segments" ("") resolve . rurl now owns dot-segment resolution (RFC 3986 §5.2.4, literal ./.. ), percent-encoded %2e treated ordinary path byte never resolved traversal — closing silent "//%2e%2e/b" → "/b" rewrite libcurl used perform. Percent-hex case still canonicalized uppercase (%2f → %2F) "keep", encoded paths remain join-equivalent. Non-compliant input handling now consistent strict (RURL-muwpjsmn). rurl longer fabricates http:// URL scheme-less input host-shaped: nonsense tokens ("asdfghjkl", "example"), free text ("hello world"), path fragments ("/relative/path") now return parse_status = "error" clean_url = NA, matching behavior inputs already errored. explicit supported scheme still trusted, "http://asdfghjkl/" remains warning--tld. Scheme-less localhost accepted (one allowlisted single-label host). IP literals validated strictly input rather trusting libcurl’s legacy inet_aton coercion. Integer, hexadecimal, octal, short-form numbers ("12345" → 0.0.48.57, "0x7f000001", "192.168"), --range wrong-arity dotted numbers ("256.1.1.1", "1.2.3.4.5"), leading-zero (octal) octets ("192.168.010.1", silently means 192.168.8.1) now return "error" instead coerced address. Canonical literals ("1.2.3.4", "[::1]") unaffected. .detect_ip_host_vec() correspondingly tightened reject zero-padded octets. http, https, ftp, ftps supported schemes (now single source truth, .SUPPORTED_SCHEMES). Scheme-bearing input scheme — opaque (mailto:, tel:, data:) authority-based unsupported (ws://, ssh://, redis://) — returns "error". New parse_status value "warning-userinfo" scheme-less input carrying userinfo (e.g. "user@example.com"): host/domain/tld/user still resolve, clean_url NA (rurl fabricate canonical URL ambiguous, email-shaped, scheme-less string). rows non-joinable canonical_join(). Input explicit scheme ("http://user@example.com") unchanged. Scheme-less user:pass@host (indistinguishable scheme:opaque) remains "error"; use scheme-relative form //user:pass@host parse .

title: rurl (development version)
text: parse pipeline split option-independent core (Stage : curl components, IP detection, post-www host, PSL domain/TLD decomposition) presentation stage (Stage B: path handling, case, host-encoding spelling, subdomain trimming, clean-URL assembly, status). full_parse cache now stores Stage , keyed URL, protocol/scheme handling, www_handling, tld_source. Calling several accessors different presentation profiles URLs (e.g. get_host(), get_domain(), get_tld(), get_clean_url(), get_subdomain()) now shares one cache entry per URL re-runs cheap Stage B, expensive curl + PSL work happens instead per profile. Output unchanged. Cache memory per URL also drops single (option-independent) entry. (RURL-dkwrebdt) safe_parse_urls() now de-duplicates input, parsing unique URL (cross-call reuse via full_parse cache) expanding results back match(). Repeated / duplicate URLs cost match, warm duplicate-heavy inputs dramatically faster. safe_parse_url() (scalar) shares cached code path. (RURL-ohepgzyf) Query-string parsing (get_query(format = "list")) now linear number key/value pairs (previously quadratic incremental list growth), URLs long query strings parse faster. Output unchanged. (RURL-actrnerd)

title: rurl (development version)
text: Accessor results (get_*()) longer named input URLs. get_*() functions now parse input single vectorized pass return plain unnamed vectors (lists), instead vectors carrying names attribute input URLs. Wrap stats::setNames(x, url) relied old names. (RURL-zpatukuq) full_parse memoization cache now bounded default 100000 unique url × option combinations (previously Inf), parsing millions unique URLs can longer grow cache without limit. Override rurl_cache_config(max_full_parse = Inf) restore previous unbounded behavior; reset-watermark semantics unchanged. (RURL-ohepgzyf) present--empty query, fragment, user, password component (e.g. query "https://example.com/?") now reported NA consistently. curl::curl_parse_url() returns components NULL libcurl versions "" others; now normalize NA, output longer depends installed libcurl version. matches behavior already produced platforms curl returned NULL. (RURL-dkwrebdt)

title: rurl (development version)
text: safe_parse_urls() now accepts factor input, coercing character labels front (matching canonical_join()), instead returning -error row every element. (RURL-actrnerd)

title: rurl (development version)
text: Scheme-less host:port input (e.g. "example.com:8080/x") longer reported parse_status = "error". parses correctly (valid host, path, clean_url) status-derivation phase mistook example.com: unsupported scheme demoted , contradicting emitted components. now reports "ok", restoring invariant present clean_url implies non-error status. Genuinely unsupported/opaque schemes (mailto:, user:pass@host) still return "error". (RURL-aldwnots) path/fragment/userinfo longer percent-decoded parsing; path_encoding = 'keep' now honors contract (leaves path byte--byte); raw query preserved (?flag stays flag, flag=). NOTE: clean_url values change URLs containing percent-encoded path bytes — since clean_url canonical_join key, /%2Fb //b longer collide. (RURL-yuozrhop)

title: rurl (development version)
text: Clarified path_normalization path_encoding docs describe normalization underlying parser (libcurl) applies rurl sees path: RFC 3986 dot-segment resolution (./.., including %2e/%2E) unconditional disabled — path_normalization = "none" still resolves //../b /b — percent-encoding hex digits normalized uppercase (%2f → %2F), RFC 3986 §6.2.2.1 case canonicalization makes paths compare equal canonical_join(). Behavior unchanged; documentation now matches . (RURL-mxoprhsy)

title: rurl 1.4.1
text: safe_parse_url()/safe_parse_urls() now recognize IPv6 address literals carry embedded dotted-quad IPv4 tail (RFC 4291 §2.2 form 3 / §2.5.5, e.g. [::ffff:127.0.0.1], [64:ff9b::8.8.8.8]). Previously fell registered-name path, returning is_ip_host = FALSE spurious warning-invalid-tld status; now report is_ip_host = TRUE parse_status = "ok". dotted hex-hextet spellings address ([::ffff:0808:0808] vs [::ffff:7f00:1]) now classify identically. malformed embedded tail (octet range) still rejected. (RURL-tvfpeocg)

title: rurl 1.4.1
text: Added dependency vulnerability audit Sonatype OSS Index via oysteR (new Suggests). tests/testthat/test-security.R runs oysteR::expect_secure("rurl") dedicated security-audit.yml workflow (weekly + demand) executes OSS Index credentials; test skips cleanly without credentials, offline, CRAN. (RURL-tyfshnat) Added second, token-free dependency vulnerability audit OSV database (https://osv.dev) via rosv (new Suggests). tests/testthat/test-osv.R checks runtime dependency closure rurl (recursive Depends + Imports) installed versions, dedicated osv-audit.yml workflow (weekly + demand) executes secrets; test skips cleanly offline CRAN. (RURL-ttkwljva)

title: rurl 1.4.0
text: pslr dependency floor now >= 1.0.2 punycoder floor >= 1.2.0. releases form coordinated punycoder 1.2.0 host-normalization API pair, fresh install pulls compatible set; rurl submitted dependency updates CRAN.

title: rurl 1.4.0
text: get_path() gains path_normalization, index_page_handling, trailing_slash_handling, path_encoding arguments, matching corresponding options safe_parse_url(). get_scheme() gains scheme_relative_handling. get_parse_status() gains source (mapped tld_source) warning statuses can queried specific PSL section. get_clean_url() get_host() gain source (mapped tld_source). get_host() gains host_encoding. get_domain(), get_tld(), get_subdomain() gain host_encoding, mirroring get_host(). new arguments default values safe_parse_url(), existing calls unaffected.

title: rurl 1.4.0
text: domain-family accessors (get_domain(), get_tld(), get_subdomain()) now follow host_encoding (default "keep") instead always returning Unicode. "keep" emitted domain/TLD/ subdomain mirrors input host’s spelling: -label (xn--…) host yields -label parts, Unicode host yields Unicode parts. Pass host_encoding = "unicode" previous always-decoded output, "idna" force -labels. makes domain accessors consistent get_host(), whose host_encoding already defaulted "keep".

title: rurl 1.4.0
text: Parse-status string literals replaced named constants (R/status-constants.R) predicates (.is_ok_status(), .is_warning_status(), .is_joinable_status()). Cache touchpoints R/zzz.R now driven single .CACHE_REGISTRY instead repeating cache names hand. Cleared lintr/goodpractice findings across R/ tests (e.g. fixed = TRUE dot splits, condition-message construction, dropped unnecessary lambdas) behavior change. .lintr now mirrors goodpractice’s linter set, local lintr::lint_package() matches goodpractice report; intentional test-idiom deviations documented config header. Restored 100% line coverage: added targeted tests .punycode_to_unicode(""), .host_is_ace(), .cache_enabled() guard branches derive_parse_status() NA-host-dot fallback (fixed -escaped regex literal left scheme-slash NA guard untested). two genuinely unreachable www-prefix regex-capture fallbacks now marked # nocov justification. Reduced cyclomatic complexity canonical_join() (47→7), get_subdomain() (26→6), rurl_cache_config() (23→5), safe_parse_urls() (19→3) extracting named sub-helpers (e.g. .cj_validate_inputs()/.cj_resolve_sides()/.cj_build_join_df(), .subdomain_labels(), .validate_max_full_parse(), .spu_coerce_original()). behavior change; function package now exceeds goodpractice cyclocomp threshold 15.

title: rurl 1.4.0
text: Added package-level documentation (?rurl / man/rurl-package.Rd) via "_PACKAGE" sentinel, maintainer ORCID, package URLs, cross-promotion pslr/punycoder now render help/landing page. Enabled roxygen2 markdown (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)), regenerating man/*.Rd (inline backticks now render \code{}). Fixed stale inst/CITATION: now reads version package metadata (hardcoded 0.2.0), uses correct title, carries maintainer ORCID. Added root CITATION.cff. Added X-schema.org-keywords, r-universe URL, codemeta.json discoverability. Maintainer email simplified bartek@turczynski.pl.

title: rurl 1.3.0
text: Public Suffix List matching now delegated pslr package (Imports: pslr (>= 1.0.1)). rurl longer ships processed copy list (R/sysdata.rda) embedded matcher, data-raw/update_psl.R removed. punycoder now required >= 1.1.0.

title: rurl 1.3.0
text: embedded matcher used 1.2.0 fully spec-correct. Delegating pslr fixes following; outputs change accordingly: Wildcard rules (*.) now honored TLD extraction. example get_tld(".b.kobe.jp") now "b.kobe.jp" ("kobe.jp"). Exception rules (!) now honored TLD extraction. example get_tld("www.ck") now "ck" ("www.ck"), get_tld("foo.ck") now "foo.ck" ("ck"). IDN hosts now resolve registered domain every section. example get_domain("example.рф") now "example.рф" (NA). safe_parse_url() / safe_parse_urls() now derive domain field using requested tld_source rather always using combined list, domain tld consistent within parse. tld_source = "private" ("icann"), host suffix section now domain = NA; consequently subdomain_levels_to_keep -op hosts (registered domain trim toward). default tld_source = "" unaffected. Hosts unknown TLD continue return NA domain TLD (rurl queries pslr unknown = "na"), rather treating unknown single label public suffix.

title: rurl 1.3.0
text: per-host domain tld memoization caches removed; pslr caches query results. rurl_cache_config() rurl_cache_info() now cover full_parse, puny_encode, puny_decode, domain / tld arguments rurl_cache_config() longer exist.

title: rurl 1.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-19

title: rurl 1.2.0
text: punycoder (used IDNA/Punycode encoding decoding) now CRAN. DESCRIPTION requires punycoder (>= 1.0.0).

title: rurl 1.2.0
text: package-wide default case_handling now "lower_host" ("keep" safe_parse_url(), safe_parse_urls(), get_clean_url(), get_*() accessors, "lower" get_path()). RFC 3986 §6.2.2.1 normalization: case-insensitive scheme host fold lowercase case-sensitive path preserved. previous defaults, hosts WWW.Example.COM www.example.com fold one identity, get_path() silently lowercased paths (two pages differ path casing collapsed one). Pass case_handling = "keep" restore previous reconstruction, "lower" lowercase whole URL including path. (RURL-lzepdnmm)

title: rurl 1.1.0
text: canonical_join() gains name_A / name_B arguments set output original-URL column names explicitly. default NULL, preserving previous deparse(substitute()) behavior; supply stable names piping passing anonymous inputs (e.g. canonical_join(df[df$x > 1, ], get_b())), otherwise produced unstable column names. (RURL-fsygrelr) canonical_join() gains join_parse_status argument controlling parse statuses yield joinable keys. default "ok" preserves previous behavior (ok* statuses join); "ok_or_warning" additionally treats parseable--suspicious warning-* statuses (warning--tld, warning-invalid-tld, warning-public-suffix) joinable, cost potential false-positive matches. (RURL-edqdrvfu) Cache introspection configuration. rurl_cache_info() reports entry count, enabled state, bound memoization cache (full_parse, domain, tld). rurl_cache_config() enables disables individual caches sets optional max_full_parse bound full-parse cache (default Inf, preserving previous unbounded behavior); bound reached cache reset peak memory stays bounded. domain tld caches remain unbounded design — grow number unique hosts, URL/option combinations — can disabled workloads many unique hosts. (RURL-iuotpaqs)

title: rurl 1.1.0
text: safe_parse_url() now returns port integer (NA_integer_), safe_parse_urls() longer errors URLs contain explicit port (e.g. http://example.com:8080/path). Previously scalar parser returned port character string vectorized parser aborted. (RURL-fxyzanfg) Bracketed IPv6 hosts (e.g. http://[2001:db8::1]/) now correctly detected IP hosts: is_ip_host TRUE, parse_status "ok", TLD/domain derivation attempted — matching IPv4 hosts already handled. -escaped detection pattern previously prevented . (RURL-jpqjndld)

title: rurl 1.1.0
text: subdomain_levels_to_keep = N (N > 0) now keeps N rightmost subdomain labels documented, instead silently retaining subdomains. example, safe_parse_url("http://deep.sub.domain.example.com", subdomain_levels_to_keep = 1) now returns host domain.example.com (deep.sub.domain.example.com). N = 0 (strip ) unchanged. Code relied previous -op behavior N > 0 see different output. (RURL-szumhumv)

title: rurl 1.1.0
text: Documented clean_url composition: normalized canonical key built scheme, host, path . Port, query, fragment, userinfo intentionally excluded, path_encoding = "decode" path shown decoded (human-readable, guaranteed URL-safe). matches existing behavior key used canonical_join() — behavior change. Corrected lower_host description implied userinfo retained clean_url, fixed README example whose input contained literal space (now percent-encoded) parses documented. (RURL-jnboujtd)

title: rurl 0.3.0
text: New case_handling trailing_slash_handling parameters safe_parse_url() get_clean_url() provide greater control URL formatting. Introduced canonical_join() joining datasets normalized URL keys. Improved handling non-standard malformed schemes like htp://. Fixed parsing schemeless URLs ports (e.g., example.com:8080/path). reliable fallback curl::curl_parse_url() fails internally. Corrected regular expressions IPv6 parsing.
